title: Scope Change Confirm Dialog
description: Second step inside [`ui:change-metadata-editor`](../change-metadata-editor/spec.md) **`ChangeScopeDialog`**: when the user saves a **scope** change (add/remove `specIds`), Studio MUST confirm before PATCH because approvals are invalidated and removed specs lose scaffolded artifacts.
context: Second step inside [`ui:change-metadata-editor`](../change-metadata-editor/spec.md) **`ChangeScopeDialog`**: when the user saves a **scope** change (add/remove `specIds`), Studio MUST confirm before PATCH because approvals are invalidated and removed specs lose scaffolded artifacts.

rules.requirement: scope confirm is a step inside ChangeScopeDialog
rules: When **Save changes** includes `addSpecIds` or `removeSpecIds`, the dialog MUST switch to a confirm view with copy from `buildScopeChangeConfirmMessage` and **Back** / **Apply scope change** actions (using shadcn `Button` components) â€” not a separate top-level modal on Overview.
When only `specDependsOn` values changed, **Save changes** MUST apply dependency PATCHes without the scope confirm step.
The confirm sub-step body MUST explain that approvals are invalidated and list specs to add (`+ id`) and remove (`âˆ’ id`) using copy from `buildScopeChangeConfirmMessage`.

scenarios.requirement: scope confirm is a step inside ChangeScopeDialog
scenarios.name: Confirm sub-step after Save when scope changed
given: user changed spec scope in studio-change-scope-dialog
then: dialog title becomes confirm scope change
AND body lists specs to add and remove
AND action buttons (Back, Apply scope change) use shadcn Button components
AND no PATCH runs until Apply scope change

scenarios.requirement: scope confirm is a step inside ChangeScopeDialog
scenarios.name: Back returns to editor
then: warning and scope/dependency editors are visible again

scenarios.name: Only dependsOn changed
then: AND dependency PATCHes may run directly

scenarios.name: Body lists adds and removes
given: user will add ui:new and remove core:old
then: AND lists + ui:new and âˆ’ core:old
